channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Emerging Australian designers spotlighted at Decor + Design 2026 in Melbourne
item.description: Decor + Design 2026, celebrating its 22nd year, is set to take place in Melbourne from 15 to 17 July, positioning itself as Australia’s leading design trade fair. The event will feature more than 200 ...

item.title: NDMA issues nationwide alert for GLOF, flash floods and landslides during active monsoon period
item.description: The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) of Pakistan has issued a comprehensive weather and disaster alert warning of increased risks of glacial lake outburst floods (GLOFs), flash floods, la...

item.title: UK forecast shows temperatures rising to 29°C amid July heatwave with regional contrasts
item.description: The UK is set to experience another spell of hot weather as temperatures rise again in a developing July heatwave, with forecasters indicating highs of around 29°C in parts of the country. According t...

item.title: Udupi resident found dead in Goa hotel room; police initiate murder investigation
item.description: A 42-year-old man from Malpe in Karnataka’s Udupi district, identified as Sandeep Saliyan, was found dead under suspicious circumstances in a hotel room in Calangute, Goa. Following the incident, Goa ...

item.title: Explore Mediterranean culture, cuisine and coastal attractions in Valencia, Spain
item.description: This sponsored travel feature highlights the Valencia region in Spain as a diverse and accessible Mediterranean destination offering a mix of cuisine, culture, history and natural beauty. Known as the...

item.title: Government restarts Sinza redevelopment plan following resident inclusion concerns
item.description: The Government has announced a restart of the Sinza Redevelopment Plan in Dar es Salaam after residents raised serious concerns over lack of proper public participation and unclear land ownership righ...

item.title: Tourist boat capsizes in Swat’s Saifullah Lake, seven dead and two rescued
item.description: A tragic boating accident occurred at Saifullah Lake on the Swat River when a tourist boat capsized, resulting in the deaths of seven people and leaving two others rescued alive. According to Rescue 1...

item.title: Sydney childcare worker sentenced to 12 years for abusing children at multiple childcare centres
item.description: A Sydney childcare worker has been sentenced to 12 years’ imprisonment, with a non-parole period of seven years, after pleading guilty to multiple serious child abuse offences committed across several...

item.title: US and Iran negotiations in Doha stall despite claims of progress from Trump administration
item.description: Indirect talks between the United States and Iran held in Doha have ended without a breakthrough, with mediators confirming that discussions concluded in a stalemate despite cautious optimism from som...

item.title: Catholic Schools NSW chief executive steps down amid ICAC investigation into electoral donation allegations
item.description: The chief executive of Catholic Schools NSW has stepped down from their position following the commencement of an investigation by the New South Wales Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) ...

item.title: Community opposition grows over proposed removal of historic Norfolk pines for Fremantle pedestrian crossings
item.description: Residents in Fremantle, Western Australia, have strongly opposed a local council proposal that could see two century-old Norfolk pine trees removed to make way for new pedestrian crossings at a busy c...

item.title: Overview of Huduma Centres in Kiambu County and the services available to residents
item.description: Kiambu County in Kenya hosts several Huduma Centres that serve as one-stop government service hubs aimed at improving access to public services. According to the report, the county currently has two o...

item.title: Convicted people smuggler identified living in the UK and reportedly seeking asylum after BBC investigation
item.description: A BBC investigation has revealed that a convicted people smuggler, described by French prosecutors as one of the most significant figures in cross-Channel trafficking networks, is currently living in ...

item.title: Tony Karon reflects on democracy, power and lessons from the anti-apartheid struggle on Crossing the Line podcast
item.description: Struggle activist turned journalist Tony Karon shares reflections on his experiences during and after the anti-apartheid movement in South Africa in a conversation on Ryland Fisher’s Crossing the Line...

item.title: Daily horoscope insights for all zodiac signs on July 2, 2026
item.description: This horoscope column for July 2, 2026 provides guidance and reflections for each zodiac sign, offering brief advice on personal growth, relationships, career decisions, and emotional well-being. It b...

item.title: The Hindu launches FIFA World Cup themed daily quiz featuring records and football trivia
item.description: The article refers to a daily quiz published by The Hindu focusing on the FIFA World Cup, designed to engage readers with football-related trivia and historical facts from the tournament’s rich legacy...

item.title: PCG issues safety advisory urging public to report suspected rocket debris and avoid contact
item.description: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has issued a public advisory reminding coastal communities, particularly in areas like Cuyo, Palawan, to immediately report any suspected rocket debris while ensuring ...

item.title: Tanzania mining revenue reaches TZS 1.39 trillion in 2025/26, surpassing government target
item.description: Tanzania’s mining sector has recorded strong performance in the 2025/26 financial year, with total revenue reaching TZS 1.394 trillion (approximately USD 558 million). This result has exceeded the gov...

item.title: SERAP Demands Investigation Into N6.3bn Constituency Project Funds or Legal Action Against National Assembly Leaders
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has called on the leadership of Nigeria’s National Assembly, specifically Senate President Godswill Akpabio and Speaker Tajudeen Abbas, to ...

item.title: Parliament moves to amend Employment Act to formally recognise remote workers in Kenya
item.description: Parliament in Kenya is considering amendments to the Employment Act to formally recognise remote workers as employees, in a move aimed at aligning the country’s labour laws with the realities of the m...
